SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.observable.micrometer

Class FailoverMeterBinder

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.observable.micrometer.FailoverMeterBinder
All Implemented Interfaces:
io.micrometer.core.instrument.binder.MeterBinder, org.springframework.beans.factory.SmartInitializingSingleton
public class FailoverMeterBinder extends Object implements io.micrometer.core.instrument.binder.MeterBinder, org.springframework.beans.factory.SmartInitializingSingleton
MeterBinder that exposes static failover configuration as Micrometer gauges.

Meters registered

  • failover.registered.total (Gauge) — total number of @Failover annotations discovered; sampled lazily so it reflects the live scanner state
  • failover.config.expiry.seconds (Gauge) — configured expiry in seconds for each named failover; tags: name, unit
  • failover.live.entries (Gauge) — current stored entry count per failover (cache footprint); tags: name, domain. Registered only when the assembled store is FailoverStoreSizeAware and supports counting: always for in-memory / Caffeine, and for JDBC only when failover.store.jdbc.live-entries-gauge-enabled=true (opt-in COUNT(*) per scrape, audit A7). Not available in multi-tenant mode

Lifecycle ordering

The binder implements SmartInitializingSingleton so per-failover expiry gauges are registered after SpringContextFailoverScanner has completed its scan. The total-count gauge is a lazy supplier — safe to register immediately in bindTo(io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry) before the scanner has finished.
Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    FailoverMeterBinder(FailoverScanner scanner, FailoverExpiryExtractor expiryExtractor)
    Creates a binder with the required scanner and expiry extractor (no live-entries gauge).
    FailoverMeterBinder(FailoverScanner scanner, FailoverExpiryExtractor expiryExtractor, @Nullable Object store)
    Creates a binder that additionally exposes the failover.live.entries gauge when the assembled store can report its size.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    afterSingletonsInstantiated()
    Registers per-failover expiry gauges after the scanner has completed its scan.
    void
    bindTo(@NonNull io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry registry)
    Registers the lazy total-count gauge and stores the registry for deferred expiry-gauge binding.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

  • Constructor Details

    • FailoverMeterBinder

      public FailoverMeterBinder(FailoverScanner scanner, FailoverExpiryExtractor expiryExtractor)
      Creates a binder with the required scanner and expiry extractor (no live-entries gauge).
      Parameters:
      scanner - scanner that provides the list of registered failovers
      expiryExtractor - extracts expiry configuration from @Failover annotations

    • FailoverMeterBinder

      public FailoverMeterBinder(FailoverScanner scanner, FailoverExpiryExtractor expiryExtractor, @Nullable Object store)
      Creates a binder that additionally exposes the failover.live.entries gauge when the assembled store can report its size.
      Parameters:
      scanner - scanner that provides the list of registered failovers
      expiryExtractor - extracts expiry configuration from @Failover annotations
      store - the assembled failover store; the live-entries gauge is registered only when it is a FailoverStoreSizeAware that supports counting

  • Method Details

    • bindTo

      public void bindTo(@NonNull io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry registry)
      Registers the lazy total-count gauge and stores the registry for deferred expiry-gauge binding. Called by Spring Boot's MeterRegistryPostProcessor for each available registry.
      Specified by:
      bindTo in interface io.micrometer.core.instrument.binder.MeterBinder

    • afterSingletonsInstantiated

      public void afterSingletonsInstantiated()
      Registers per-failover expiry gauges after the scanner has completed its scan. Fires after all singleton beans are instantiated.
      Specified by:
      afterSingletonsInstantiated in interface org.springframework.beans.factory.SmartInitializingSingleton
